title: rosa × engelmannii nothosubsp. occidentalis
intro: Rosa × engelmannii nothosubsp. occidentalis, also known as Engelmann's wild rose or Western wild rose, is a hybrid plant species belonging to the Rosa genus. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, inflammation, and skin conditions due to its high concentration of salicylic acid and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Rosa × engelmannii nothosubsp. occidentalis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
